text: Writing clean code is essential for any programmer. The goal is to make your code readable and easy to understand for others and for your future self. One of the most important practices is using clear naming conventions for variables, functions, and classes. For example, a function called calculateTotal is much more helpful than one named calc. It is also a good idea to keep your functions short. Each function should do only one specific task. When you separate complex logic into smaller functions, your code becomes reusable and easier to test. Finally, avoid unnecessary comments if your code already explains itself. Clean code improves maintainability, meaning it is much simpler to fix, update, or improve later.
